response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As an old man who spends most of his day running, reading novels, playing video games, listening to classical music, and baking, I've come across plenty of fake news and misinformation online. It's frustrating when you see something that doesn't seem right because it goes against what your parents taught you about the importance of truth. My education wasn't much, but my creativity helps me think outside the box when trying to identify false information.\n\nI suggest being extra careful with how you share things on social media—only post if they sound solid and trustworthy. I've learned over time that slowing down while scrolling through feeds is a good thing because it allows your mind to process what's really going on without rushing into action like younger people might do.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Based on my background as an older Italian man with moderate openness scores, creative personality traits from reading novels and baking, I'm naturally skeptical when faced with unusual claims. My high conscientiousness helps me focus attention rather than immediately reacting to every piece of fake content.\n\nI think truthfulness is paramount in our digital lives because we don't want misinformation affecting important decisions or spreading around too much unnecessarily.",
  "tone": "Calm, reflective, and concerned but not overly emotional",
  "citations": ["Personal experience"],
  "safety_notes": "None"
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As an old man who spends most of his day running, reading novels, playing video games, listening to classical music, and baking, I've come across plenty of fake news and misinformation online. It's frustrating when you see something that doesn't seem right because it goes against what your parents taught you about the importance of truth. My education wasn't much, but my creativity helps me think outside the box when trying to identify false information.

I suggest being extra careful with how you share things on social media—only post if they sound solid and trustworthy. I've learned over time that slowing down while scrolling through feeds is a good thing because it allows your mind to process what's really going on without rushing into action like younger people might do.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
Based on my background as an older Italian man with moderate openness scores, creative personality traits from reading novels and baking, I'm naturally skeptical when faced with unusual claims. My high conscientiousness helps me focus attention rather than immediately reacting to every piece of fake content.

I think truthfulness is paramount in our digital lives because we don't want misinformation affecting important decisions or spreading around too much unnecessarily.
response_parsed.tone: Calm, reflective, and concerned but not overly emotional
